subject: Product Management
title: Google Wave
description: Learn Google Wave in one minute.

hook.text: Google Wave reimagined email, chat, and documents as one real-time collaborative platform. Nobody understood what it was.

buildup.text: Launched in 2009, Wave let multiple people edit a conversation-document hybrid simultaneously. It was technically brilliant.

discovery.text: The product tried to replace email, instant messaging, and wikis — all at once. Users didn't know which problem it solved.

twist.text: A decade later, Slack, Notion, and Google Docs each solved one piece of what Wave attempted. Separately, each succeeded.

climax.text: Wave failed because it asked users to change too many behaviors simultaneously. People adopt change one step at a time.

punchline.text: Solve one problem brilliantly. Not three problems confusingly.

quiz.question: Why did Google Wave fail?
options: It tried to replace too many things at once — users didn't know which problem it solved
It was too expensive for most users
